title: Dogfood sync: re-onboard the bee repo so vendored helpers and AGENTS block pick up harness10
action: Run the onboarding plan on the bee repo itself (node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --json), summarize the changes — vendored helper/lib updates from harness10 template edits, AGENTS block update from harness10-7, AND the expected propose_agents_header item (this repo AGENTS.md is block-only, so the D4 audit fires by design; the applied header with its [unknown] fill-me line is intended dogfood output). Re-run with --apply. Then seed docs/backlog.md through the scribing Backlog rules harness10-7 just wrote: three proposed rows from CONTEXT.md Deferred Ideas (init lane for greenfield repos; PBI priority scoring; README badges), IDs from 1, priority order as listed, Feature column empty. Confirm: recheck up_to_date; node .bee/bin/bee_status.mjs --json includes real pbi counts (3 proposed); node .bee/bin/lib/commands_detect.mjs prints JSON candidates (run AFTER apply — the file is vendored by apply). Standard vendored-sync cell (harness09 precedent) + first real PBI capture.

truths: Onboarding recheck after apply reports up_to_date
bee_status --json includes pbi with 3 proposed rows counted from the seeded docs/backlog.md
The vendored commands_detect.mjs CLI prints JSON candidates from the repo root (after apply)
The applied AGENTS.md header outside the markers carries the [unknown] fill-me line (D4 dogfood)
prohibitions: No manual edits to vendored files - onboarding apply is the only write path for .bee/bin and AGENTS.md
No changes outside .bee/bin, AGENTS.md (BEE block + the D4-proposed header, expected on this block-only file), onboarding.json, and the seeded docs/backlog.md

verify: node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --json | grep -q up_to_date && node .bee/bin/bee_status.mjs --json | grep -q '"pbi"' && node .bee/bin/lib/commands_detect.mjs | grep -qF '[' && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs

trace.outcome: Dogfood sync complete: vendored .bee/bin picks up harness10 templates (backlog, commands_detect, cells pbi field, inject PBI line, bee_status pbi), AGENTS.md gained D4-proposed header with [unknown] fill-me line, docs/backlog.md seeded with 3 proposed PBI rows. Verify green (chain exit 0). Block was an orchestrator-authored verify-string bug (grep -q '[' invalid regex), fixed to grep -qF.
trace.verify_output: onboard: up_to_date | pbi={proposed:3,in_flight:0,done:0} | commands_detect: [] | test_lib 59/0 | test_onboard PASS | CHAIN EXIT 0
trace.verify_command: onboard up_to_date && bee_status pbi && commands_detect [] && test_lib && test_onboard
trace.blocked_reason: Cell verify command is broken on this platform: segment 3 'node .bee/bin/lib/commands_detect.mjs | grep -q [' uses an unmatched-bracket regex, which GNU grep rejects as 'Invalid regular expression' (exit 2), aborting the && chain before the two test suites. Fix is one character in the verify STRING: grep -qF '[' (or grep -q '\['). The verify string is in the cell JSON, outside this cell's write scope (prohibition: no changes outside .bee/bin, AGENTS.md, onboarding.json, docs/backlog.md). All four must-have truths independently PASS; the dogfood work (apply + backlog seed) is complete and correct.
